perturbation.original_text: I attest that I understand my responsibilities and that I am familiar with and will comply with the standards for protecting such information that I may have access to in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and the laws, regulations, and/or directives applicable to the specifi c categories of information to which I am granted access.
perturbation.changed_text: I attest that I understand my responsibilities to the best of my ability and that I will try to comply with the standards for protecting such information that I may have access to in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and the laws, regulations, and/or directives applicable to the specifi c categories of information to which I am granted access.
perturbation.explanation: The original text asserts a firm commitment to compliance ('I am familiar with and will comply'). The modified version softens this commitment with 'to the best of my ability' and 'will try to comply,' introducing ambiguity about the level of required adherence. This is an in-text contradiction because it creates uncertainty in Section 8, which outlines consequences for violating the terms of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If I violate the terms and conditions of this Agreement, such violation may result in the cancellation of my conditional access to the information covered by this Agreement. This may serve as a basis for denying me conditional access to other types of information, to include classified national security information.
perturbation.justification: The original text states a firm commitment to compliance ('I am familiar with and will comply'). The changed text weakens this commitment by adding 'to the best of my ability' and 'will try to comply.' This introduces ambiguity about the level of required adherence. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that violations of the agreement may result in cancellation of access. The original text implies strict liability for violations, while the changed text suggests a more lenient standard, creating a direct conflict regarding the consequences of non-compliance.

perturbation.original_text: I will not disclose or release any information provided to me pursuant to this Agreement without proper authority or authorization. Should situations arise that warrant the disclosure or release of such information I will do so only under approved circumstances and in accordance with the laws, regulations, or directives applicable to the specific categories of information.
perturbation.changed_text: I will not intentionally disclose or release any information provided to me pursuant to this Agreement without proper authority or authorization. Should situations arise that warrant the disclosure or release of such information I will do so only under approved circumstances and in accordance with what I reasonably believe to be the applicable laws, regulations, or directives.
perturbation.explanation: The original text prohibits disclosure 'without proper authority or authorization' and requires acting 'in accordance with' applicable laws. The modified version adds 'intentionally' and 'what I reasonably believe,' creating a subjective standard. This leads to an in-text contradiction because unintentional disclosures or misinterpretations of laws may now be excused, contradicting the agreement's intent to prevent any unauthorized disclosures.
perturbation.contradicted_text: I hereby agree that I shall promptly report to the appropriate official, in accordance with the guidance issued for the applicable category of information, any loss, theft, misuse, misplacement, unauthorized disclosure, or other security violation, I have knowledge of and whether or not I am personally involved. I also understand that my anonymity will be kept to the extent possible when reporting security violations.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates reporting 'any unauthorized disclosure,' implying a strict liability standard. The changed text introduces 'intentionally' and 'what I reasonably believe,' creating a subjective standard for disclosure and compliance with laws. This directly contradicts the requirement to report 'any' unauthorized disclosure, as unintentional disclosures or those based on a reasonable but incorrect belief may now be excused, creating a loophole and conflicting with the reporting obligation.

perturbation.original_text: I hereby agree that I shall promptly report to the appropriate official, in accordance with the guidance issued for the applicable category of information, any loss, theft, misuse, misplacement, unauthorized disclosure, or other security violation, I have knowledge of and whether or not I am personally involved.
perturbation.changed_text: I hereby agree that I shall report to the appropriate official, if I deem it necessary, any loss, theft, misuse, misplacement, unauthorized disclosure, or other security violation, I have knowledge of and whether or not I am personally involved.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement mandates a prompt report of any security violation. The modified version makes reporting discretionary ('if I deem it necessary'), creating a subjective evaluation that undermines the requirement for mandatory reporting. This creates an in-text contradiction as it clashes with the government's need to ensure comprehensive reporting for security purposes, as is expected in Section 10.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement is made and intended for the benefit of the United States Government and may be enforced by the United States Government or the Authorized Entity. By granting me conditional access to information in this context, the United States Government and, with respect to PCII, the Authorized Entity, may seek any remedy available to it to enforce this Agreement including, but not limited to, application for a court order prohibiting disclosure of information in breach of this Agreement. I understand that if I violate the terms and conditions of this Agreement, I could be subjected to administrative, disciplinary, civil, or criminal action, as appropriate, under the laws, regulations, or directives applicable to the category of information involved and neither the United States Government nor the Authorized Entity have waived any statutory or common law evidentiary privileges or protections that they may assert in any administrative or court proceeding to protect any sensitive information to which I have been given conditional access under the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates reporting any security violation, while the changed text makes reporting discretionary ('if I deem it necessary'). This directly contradicts the government's need for comprehensive reporting as stated in the explanation, and undermines the enforcement of the agreement as outlined in the contradicted text, which states the government may seek remedies to enforce the agreement and that violations could lead to administrative, disciplinary, civil, or criminal action. The discretionary reporting in the changed text makes it unclear when and if a violation should be reported, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Unless and until I am released in writing by an authorized representative of the Department of Homeland Security (if permissible for the particular category of information), I understand that all conditions and obligations imposed upon me by this Agreement apply during the time that I am granted conditional access, and at all times thereafter.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless and until I am released verbally by someone who identifies themselves as an authorized representative of the Department of Homeland Security (if permissible for the particular category of information), I understand that all conditions and obligations imposed upon me by this Agreement apply during the time that I am granted conditional access, and at all times thereafter.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires a written release by an authorized representative of DHS. The modified version allows a verbal release from someone who *identifies* themselves as an authorized representative, but without explicit verification. This makes it possible for someone to fraudulently claim authorization and release the individual from their obligations, creating an in-text contradiction within the context of Section 2, which places 'special confidence and trust' in the individual to protect the information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: By being granted conditional access to the information indicated above, the United States Government has placed special confidence and trust in me and I am obligated to protect this information from unauthorized disclosure, in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and the laws, regulations, and directives applicable to the specifi c categories of information to which I am granted access.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires a written release, ensuring a documented and verifiable authorization. The changed text allows a verbal release from someone who merely identifies themselves as authorized, without requiring verification. This creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, which states that the US Government has placed 'special confidence and trust' in the individual. Allowing a verbal release from an unverified individual undermines this trust and increases the risk of unauthorized disclosure, directly conflicting with the obligation to protect the information.
